q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: The sink is an f-string interpolation into an HTML string on line 47; this is pure string formatting with no network I/O, shell execution, DB write, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The unescaped user-controlled value is embedded directly in the returned string, so the XSS violation is fully present in the return value.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and returns.exprs contains the f-string expression, confirming the result is directly accessible as a string in memory on return.
